meta.title: AWS vs GCP for FinTech: Cloud Platform Comparison
meta.description: Structured comparison of Amazon Web Services and Google Cloud Platform for fintech startups and enterprises, covering compliance, managed services, pricing, and ML capabilities.

aws.notes: PCI DSS Level 1, SOC 1/2/3, ISO 27001, FedRAMP High, GDPR, HIPAA BAA; most mature compliance posture

gcp.notes: PCI DSS Level 1, SOC 1/2/3, ISO 27001, FedRAMP High, GDPR, HIPAA BAA; comparable to AWS

aws.notes: RDS (PostgreSQL, MySQL, Oracle, MSSQL), Aurora, DynamoDB, ElastiCache, Redshift, DocumentDB

gcp.notes: Cloud SQL, AlloyDB (PostgreSQL-compatible), Spanner (global), Bigtable, Firestore, BigQuery

aws.notes: Lambda, API Gateway, SQS, SNS, EventBridge, Step Functions; most mature serverless platform

gcp.notes: Cloud Functions, Cloud Run, Pub/Sub, Workflows, Eventarc; Cloud Run excels for containerized functions

dimensions.name: Data analytics for risk and fraud

aws.notes: Redshift, Glue, Athena, Kinesis; mature but more setup required

gcp.notes: BigQuery serverless DW; Dataflow for streaming; Looker for BI; best-in-class for analytics at scale

aws.notes: SageMaker (MLOps), Bedrock (LLMs), Rekognition, Fraud Detector, Textract; broad but fragmented

gcp.notes: Vertex AI (unified ML platform), TPUs, Gemini API, Contact Center AI; strongest ML infrastructure

aws.notes: VPC, CloudFront CDN, Direct Connect, Transit Gateway, PrivateLink; largest and most mature

gcp.notes: VPC (global by default), Cloud CDN, Interconnect, Cloud Armor DDoS; premium network tier is best latency

aws.notes: EKS (managed K8s), ECR, ECS (proprietary), Fargate; EKS control plane costs $0.10/hr

gcp.notes: GKE (inventor of Kubernetes); Autopilot mode; Artifact Registry; tightest K8s integration

aws.notes: GuardDuty, Security Hub, Macie, Inspector, Shield, WAF, KMS, CloudTrail; deepest security toolset

gcp.notes: Security Command Center, Chronicle SIEM, Cloud Armor, Secret Manager, DLP API; improving rapidly

aws.notes: Complex pricing; savings plans and reserved instances help; Cost Explorer available

gcp.notes: Sustained use discounts automatic; committed use contracts; often cheaper for compute-heavy workloads

aws.notes: Largest marketplace; 10,000+ ISV integrations; every fintech vendor certifies AWS first

gcp.notes: Growing marketplace; 2,000+ solutions; fewer niche fintech ISV integrations than AWS

aws.notes: Enterprise Support from $15,000/mo; broad SLA coverage; 24/7 TAM access

gcp.notes: Premium Support comparable; Technical Account Manager; 99.99% SLAs on key services

aws.notes: AWS certifications most common; largest pool of certified engineers globally

gcp.notes: Smaller certified talent pool; GCP skills increasingly in demand but still behind AWS

use_case_recommendations.reason: Largest fintech partner ecosystem, Fraud Detector, mature compliance tooling, most ISV certifications
Vertex AI, BigQuery ML, and Dataflow streaming pipeline are best-in-class for this use case
BigQuery serverless analytics at scale is superior to Redshift for ad-hoc query performance
Largest certified talent pool, most mature migration tooling, strongest enterprise support contracts
Vertex AI and TPU access give GCP an edge for training and serving custom financial ML models
Broadest ecosystem means most tools and partners will integrate AWS-first
